text: You dream of dancing in a palace made of ice and feasting on delicate sugar candies in the shapes of birds and fish.
You dream of riding at the head of a hunting party, the hounds panting as they chase after fleeing humanoid forms.
You dream of a duel in the rain, fought with swords made of lightning.
You dream of sitting on a throne as criminals are brought before you for judgement.
You dream of changing colors with the leaves and letting the wind come shake you down.
You dream of oaths, vows, and promises made to the seasons, tides, and moon.
In your dream, the bonfire speaks your name and tells you secrets it has learned.
You dream of stepping in between the standing stones and walking the path to the Dying Lands.
You dream of secret councils meeting around a brazier filled with frozen flames.
You dream of repairing shoes.
You dream of a well-kept house, everything in its place.
You dream of spinning straw into gold.
In your dreams, you watch from above the fire in the hearth as the children play with blocks and the dog sleeps in the kitchen.
You dream of creeping through the halls while everyone is asleep, making sure everything is in order.
You dream of eating delicious crusty bread and washing it down with fresh milk, set out just for you.
You dream of stealing all the buttons from an ungrateful mortal's clothes.
You dream of putting on the coat the householders left for you walking out the door for the last time.
You dream of finding a farmhand sleeping on the job and tying his shoes together.
You dream of flying through the night on raven's wings.
You dream of running on all fours through a field of wheat.
You dream of tracking a badger's scent back to its lair.
You dream of a raven landing on your outstretched arm and telling you what it saw while on the wing.
You dream of branches whipping your face as you sprint through the forest, away from the crashing sounds behind you.
You dream of perching in a tree while an unsuspecting group of mortals passes by underneath.
You dream of giving a message to a squirrel and watching as it scampers off to deliver it.
In your dreams, you sit in a parliament of owls, debating issues of the forest.
You dream of looking up at the sun from beneath the surface of the water.
You dream of frolicking in the seafoam.
You dream of slipping on your skin and the shiver as your skin turns to fur and your hands to flippers.
You dream of bumping a fishing boat and giggling as the rod falls in the water.
You dream of singing to the clouds and watching lightning bolts crash through the heavens.
You dream of following schools of fish, herding them like a shepherd.
You dream of a walk along the beach under the moon.
You dream of a mortal holding your skin, gloating at you as you step away from the sea.
You dream of running as a hound and shifting back as you leap for the kill.
Your dreams are filled with the round of pickaxes striking rock.
You dream of finding a rich vein of ore.
You dream of watching the moon set from the mouth of a cave.
You dream of watching a pair of humans pass by, oblivious to you among the rocks.
You dream of tapping out a message on the rock wall and listening for the reply.
Your dreams are filled with the glow of hot metal and the pounding of hammers.
You dream of the darkness and silence within the depths of the earth.
You dream of holding a finished blade, the work of months of hard labor.
You dream of mining songs unheard by mortal ears.
